name: core
description: v2.6.2 ships D36+D37 (founder directives 2026-04-27, decoupled by mechanism). D36 Feedback Channel three-layer defense after a real client session bypassed /core:feedback --public via direct `gh issue create` when the binary failed on a non-existent `feedback,vunknown` label: (a) routing-rule.sh strengthened with explicit no-gh-fallback clause during binary error recovery; (b) NEW feedback-channel-guard.sh PreToolUse:Bash hook blocks `gh (issue|pr) (create|comment|review)` against any sankalpasawa/sutra* repo with exit 2 (12/12 tests; SUTRA_FEEDBACK_GUARD_DISABLED=1 kill-switch); (c) feedback.sh binary --label flag dropped, version moved to title `[feedback v${PLUGIN_VERSION}] from plugin`. D37 Sutra Assistant Layer: paused, default-OFF — polarity flipped in assistant-kill-switch.sh, opt-in via ~/.sutra-assistant-enabled or SUTRA_ASSISTANT_ENABLED; hard kill-switches still win. bin/sutra `enable`/`disable` rewritten. Bug list B1-B8 captured in holding/research/2026-04-24-assistant-layer-design.md §0 — re-enable requires clearing those before `sutra enable`. v2.6.1 refines Trust Mode `gh`/`git` to a catastrophic-only rule per founder direction ("keep it simple — only delete"): `gh` removed from REMOTE_TOOLS, prompts only on delete-class actions (`gh repo delete`, `gh secret delete`/`remove`, `gh release delete`, `gh issue delete`, `gh codespace delete`, `gh label delete`, `gh extension remove`, `gh gist delete`, `gh variable delete`); `git` simplified to prompt only on `push --force`/`-f`/`--force-with-lease` and `clean -f*`. Everything else (gh pr create/edit/merge/close/comment, issue create/comment, repo create/edit/fork, secret set, auth login/logout, workflow run, gh api any-method; git commit/push/pull/rebase/merge/reset --hard/checkout/branch -D/tag -d/stash drop/rm/mv) auto-approves — all recoverable via reflog, remote, or fs. 156/156 unit tests green. Supersedes the initial v2.6.1 granular spec (36431a8) per 16af031. v2.6.0 ships PROTO-024 V1 client→team feedback fanout: /core:feedback now scrubs locally and pushes to sankalpasawa/sutra-data clients/<install_id>/feedback/ (collaborator-visible inbox, V2 will encrypt). Strengthened scrub_text() with 12+ token detectors (GitHub/OpenAI/AWS/Slack/Stripe/JWT/signed-URL/DSN/webhook/email/phone) plus 40+ char high-entropy fallback. Decoupled /core:feedback from SUTRA_TELEMETRY=0. push.sh stops writing manifest.identity (closes v1.9 PII leak). reset-turn-markers.sh moved from UserPromptSubmit→Stop event (closes spoof vulnerability — no content pattern detection needed). Codex review 2026-04-25 directive 1777062127+1777058308. Governance preserved end-to-end. v2.5.0 adds Tier 1.6 Trust Mode (denylist of 6 prompt categories: git mutations, privilege, recursive delete outside safe paths, disk/system, fetch-and-exec, remote/shared-state). Auto-approves everything else. Per founder approval-fatigue feedback + Codex MODIFY->GO. Operating system for building with AI. v2.1.1 patch (fleet feedback + codex review 2026-04-25): (a) /core:start project-root guard — refuses activation in $HOME or non-project dirs (no .git / package.json / pyproject.toml / Cargo.toml / go.mod / CLAUDE.md), canonical path compare prevents symlink/trailing-slash bypass, .git-as-file recognized for worktrees/submodules. --force override. Prevents home-dir poisoning of ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md with project-scoped governance. Covered by tests/unit/test-start-guard.sh (10 assertions). (b) /core:feedback docs synced to v2.1 reality — --public flag to gh CLI documented (was already wired but commands/feedback.md + usage help still said 'NOT YET IMPLEMENTED'). (c) hooks/reset-turn-markers.sh: root-cause fix — treats empty PROMPT as synthetic turn (was falling through to wipe, caused 2052:1 wipe:skip ratio blocking multi-tool Sutra edits). v2.0 — privacy model replaced: signals-not-content capture, consent-gated disk writes, T4 in-memory-default. User surface = single PRIVACY.md sheet + SUTRA_TELEMETRY=0 opt-out + /sutra feedback manual channel. Legacy v1.9 identity stamping + outbound push DEPRECATED, gated behind SUTRA_LEGACY_TELEMETRY=1. Governance surface preserved: input routing, depth estimation, readability gate, output trace, session-retrieve, per-profile enforcement, C3c token-compression, permission-gate, Bash prompt summarizer, feedback-routing-rule. Codex-reviewed DIRECTIVE-ID 1777065370 (CHANGES-REQUIRED → all P1/P2 findings absorbed).

profile.title: Enforcement profile
profile.description: individual = warn-only, telemetry off. project = warn-only, telemetry on (default). company = HARD block on missing depth markers, telemetry on.
